title: The Instagram Spiral

story.opening: <break time="400ms"/>Instagram almost ended my creative life. <break time="350ms"/>I'm not being dramatic.
story.body: <break time="350ms"/>I'd scroll through artists I followed. <break time="300ms"/>Beautiful work. <break time="250ms"/>Thousands of likes. <break time="200ms"/>Shows, commissions, success. <break time="500ms"/>And with every scroll, <break time="400ms"/>I felt myself shrink. <break time="350ms"/>My work wasn't that good. <break time="300ms"/>My colors weren't that bold. <break time="250ms"/>I didn't have their natural talent. <break time="500ms"/>I'd close the app feeling physically sick. <break time="400ms"/>Like I'd been poisoned. <break time="350ms"/>And then I'd open it again an hour later. <break time="300ms"/>Needing to know how far behind I was. <break time="500ms"/>It took me years to realize <break time="400ms"/>I was comparing my practice to their highlights.
story.reflection: <break time="500ms"/>I unfollowed everyone. <break time="400ms"/>Every artist. <break time="350ms"/>Not because they weren't good - <break time="300ms"/>because my brain couldn't handle the comparison. <break time="500ms"/>Your creative journey is yours. <break time="400ms"/>It happens at your pace. <break time="350ms"/>In your style. <break time="300ms"/>Comparison isn't information - <break time="250ms"/>it's poison.

when_to_share: When someone is spiraling in comparison to other creatives
